quick_skim.title: Structural Bottlenecks on Frequency Representation in End-to-End Audio Models
quick_skim.abstract: End-to-end neural audio models achieve high-fidelity compression and generation. We might read that performance as evidence they directly represent interpretable features such as pitch and timbre, but a model can produce plausible outputs without doing so. A model may encode these features in any reachable basis, but regardless of which, the features are well described as compositions of time-frequency-localized primitives. Whether state-of-the-art encoders preserve access to these primitives, and thus to compositions of them, remains unclear. Through theoretical analysis and controlled experiments, we show that several state-of-the-art strided convolutional encoders impose two structural bottlenecks, both predictable from architecture and signal structure, on access to these primitives: (1) they collapse primitives into alias equivalence classes, establishing a bound on representational capacity, and (2) they limit the frequency resolution available to learned filters, restricting separability. For well structured data, we find collapse rates of 31-35% and filter bandwidths 10-35x above the theoretical resolution bound, confirming that both bottlenecks arise under realistic signal conditions. We then introduce Gabor Latent Refactorization (GLRF), a lightweight post-hoc intervention that re-expresses encoder latents in a frequency-localized basis, reducing filter bandwidths from 10-35x to 1.5-3x of the theoretical resolution bound while preserving reconstruction fidelity and improving control over attributes like pitch. These results show that the encoders in question predictably degrade access to frequency-localized primitives, entangling the features that depend on them, and that a lightweight, retraining-free intervention can recover much of that access, improving steerability and interpretability.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: Analyzes frequency representation bottlenecks in audio models, informing high-frequency feature extraction
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 分析音频模型频率表征瓶颈，指导高频特征提取
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 分析音频模型频率表征瓶颈，指导高频特征提取
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 分析音频模型频率表征瓶颈，指导高频特征提取
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Shows that strided convolutional encoders impose structural bottlenecks on frequency representation, limiting access to time-frequency primitives, which has implications for high-frequency feature extraction in audio recognition models.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 针对端到端神经音频模型可能不直接保留可解释频率特征的问题，本文通过理论分析和控制实验，证明若干主流的步进卷积编码器存在两个结构性频率表征瓶颈。这些瓶颈限制了模型对时频基元的访问，从而影响高频特征的提取。该发现对基于高频特征的音频识别任务具有重要指导意义，启发设计更有效的频率表示架构。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 针对端到端神经音频模型可能不直接保留可解释频率特征的问题，本文通过理论分析和控制实验，证明若干主流的步进卷积编码器存在两个结构性频率表征瓶颈。这些瓶颈限制了模型对时频基元的访问，从而影响高频特征的提取。该发现对基于高频特征的音频识别任务具有重要指导意义，启发设计更有效的频率表示架构。
quick_skim.matched_query_text: Papers central to 在水声信号如船舰噪声中利用高频信息做识别的文章, especially work that connects or combines: high frequency components in ship noise for recognition; underwater acoustic target recognition using high frequency features; vessel noise classification with high frequency cues; high frequency acoustic features for ship noise recognition; ship radiated noise high frequency recognition; underwater acoustic signal processing for high frequency target recognition; high frequency ship noise recognition methods; using high frequency acoustic features for underwater target classification; ship radiated noise high frequency components for identification.
